display.purpose: Auth screens and rare hero moments; not used inside the app shell.

title.purpose: Card titles, section headers, dialog titles.

body.purpose: The app base size: form values, table cells, descriptions. 65–75ch max for prose.

label.purpose: Field labels, badges, table headers, metadata.

shadows.name: primary-lift
shadows.purpose: Primary button only — a faint violet glow that signals 'press me.'

shadows.name: floating-layer
shadows.purpose: Transient overlays: popovers, dropdown menus, modals, the auth card.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for entrances/state — calm exponential ease-out, no bounce.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one confident action on a screen — Signal Violet with a faint violet lift.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet companion action — muted fill, hairline border that strengthens on hover.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Lowest-emphasis action — no fill until hover; for toolbars and row actions.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Muted-fill field that 'wakes up' to a white surface + violet border on focus.

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat at rest — Pure Surface + hairline border on the Paper page. Never nested, never shadowed at rest.

components.name: Status Badge (Paid)
components.description: Status pill — low-tint background + matching ink + an icon. Never color alone.

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar/top nav link — Slate at rest, Ink on hover, Signal Violet + violet wash when active.

narrative.overview: LoomLance is the control room where a freelance developer runs the business of their business — clients, contracts, invoices, time, money. Everything is legible at a glance, status is always unambiguous, and nothing competes for attention. The operator stays calm because the surface is calm. Light-first on cool near-white neutrals (deliberately not warm cream), one confident violet voice, a tight status palette, flat-by-default depth, and a single typeface (Outfit) carrying the whole hierarchy. A full dark theme mirrors every token.
keyCharacteristics: Cool near-white neutrals (never cream/warm); the data, not the chrome, is the hero.
One confident violet voice + a tight status palette; both light and dark hold WCAG AA.
Flat by default; shadows are a response to state, not decoration.
One typeface (Outfit), full hierarchy by weight/size; numbers tabular and aligned.
Refined, restrained controls (6–8px radii, muted-fill inputs, understated hovers).

rules.name: The Confident-Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Violet is the only brand color and always means actionable/active. Use it freely across actions, links, selection, and data emphasis — never decoratively, never a second brand hue beside it.

rules.name: The Cool-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Neutrals tint toward blue (hue ~230) or stay zero-chroma. Cream/sand/beige/warm-gray is forbidden — it reads as generic-AI.

rules.name: The Status-Never-Alone Rule
rules.body: A status color is always reinforced by an icon, label, or text. Color alone never conveys state.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Outfit carries the entire hierarchy. Never add a second display or body face; hierarchy is weight and size.

rules.name: The Tabular-Number Rule
rules.body: Money, hours, dates, invoice numbers use tabular figures and right-align in tables.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Cards, panels, rows, and inputs are flat at rest — border + tone only. Shadows are reserved for what's actionable (primary button) or transient (overlays).

dos: Do keep surfaces flat at rest — border + tone, not shadow. Raise only what's actionable or transient.
Do let Signal Violet (#6D45F0) carry one meaning — actionable/active — used confidently across actions, links, selection, and data emphasis.
Do keep neutrals cool (hue ~230) or zero-chroma; the page background is Paper (#FBFBFD), a true near-white.
Do make numbers tabular and right-aligned in tables; money/hours/dates are first-class content.
Do carry the full type hierarchy in Outfit via weight and size; cap prose at 65–75ch.
Do hold WCAG AA in light and dark: body ≥4.5:1 (Ink/Slate), visible focus ring, reduced-motion alternatives, status reinforced by icon/label.
donts: Don't ship the generic SaaS template — no gradient hero, no identical icon-card grids, no Bootstrap-default spacing.
Don't recreate cluttered enterprise accounting — no dropdown soup, no dense intimidating forms, no joyless gray walls.
Don't use warm neutrals — no cream, sand, beige, parchment, or warm-gray.
Don't use Fog (#8A95A5) for body or essential text — hints/placeholders/disabled only (does not meet AA as body).
Don't introduce a second brand color or a second typeface.
Don't use a border-left/right color stripe, gradient text, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't shadow a resting card or nest cards inside cards.
Don't convey status with color alone.
